title: Method for detection of organic molecules using nuclear magnetic resonance spectroscopy assisted by gold nanoparticles
abstract: The present invention relates to a novel method for detecting and quantifying a chemical compound in complex mixtures using the nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) spectroscopy assisted by gold nanoparticles. The analyte is identified using its ability to interact with non-covalent interactions with functionalized gold nanoparticles. The detection of these compounds can occur both in organic solvent and in aqueous solvent. The analyte can be unambiguously identified and quantified, even in very complex mixtures, recording it NMR signal only. Of particular interest to the present invention are analytes containing a positively charged functional group such as ammonium organic salts and analogues of synthetic drugs.
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . A method for the detection of organic molecules in a sample comprising one or more organic compounds, using magnetization transfer nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) spectroscopy, wherein the chemical species acting as stationary phase for the detection of the organic molecules consists of Au nanoparticles functionalized on the surface with a monolayer of thiol residues, and comprising at least the steps of: magnetizing a sample comprising the sample being examined and functionalized Au nanoparticles by the application of nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) pulses; removing the magnetization of one or more organic compounds of the sample by the application of a diffusion filter; transferring the magnetization from the functionalized Au nanoparticles to one or more organic compounds of the sample being examined; removing residual magnetization of functionalized Au nanoparticles by the application of a sequence of "Carr-Purcell-Meiboom-Gill modified with a filter-z" (CPMGz) pulses which acts as a filter for transverse relaxation times T2, and recording a spectrum of signals resulting from the residual magnetization of one or more organic compounds of the sample being examined. 2. The method for the detection of organic molecules in a sample comprising one or more organic compounds according to claim 1 , wherein the Au nanoparticles are functionalized with a monolayer of thiol residues represented by the general formula (I) -S-(A Y B)p-X (I) wherein: A is selected from -CH2-, -CF2- or a combination thereof and n is an integer comprised from 6 to 12; 

 B is selected from -CH2-, -CF2-, an aryl residue or a combination thereof and p is an integer comprised from 0 to 4; Y is selected from an amide group -CON H-, urea -N HCON H-, thiourea -N HCSN H-, a dimethyl silane group -Si(CH3)2- an ether - O- group or a combination thereof and m is an integer equal to 0 or 1 ; X is selected from a macrocyclic compound of general formula - (ZW)q- where Z is selected from -CH2CH2- or -CeH 4 - or a combination thereof, W is selected from O, N , S and q is an integer comprised from 4 to 8, a carboxyl group, a phosphoric group, a phosphonic group, a sulfonic group, a sulfuric group, an amino group, an amide group, a guanidine group, a pyridinium group, an urea group or a thiourea group. The method for detecting organic molecules in a sample comprising one or more organic compounds according to claim 2, wherein in the thiol residue of general formula (I), A is a linear alkyl chain, Y is an amide group -CON H- , B with p different from 0 is a short linear alkyl chain or an aryl and X is a crown ether macrocycle, a pyridinium group or a sulfonic group. The method for the detection of organic molecules in a sample comprising one or more organic compounds according to claim 3, wherein the thiol residue of general formula (I) is selected from the residues with: A is equal to -(CH 2 )7- Y is equal to -CON H-, B is equal to -CH2- and X is a heterocycle 18-crown-6 (-S-(CH 2 )7-CON H-CH2-18- crown-6); A is equal to -(CH2)7- Y is equal to -CON H-, and X is a heterocycle dibenzo-18-crown-6 (-S-(CH-CON H-CH 2 -dibenzo-18-crown-6); A is equal to -(CH 2 )n- and X is -SO3 (-S-(CH 2 )n- SO3-); A is equal to -(CH2)7-, Y is equal to -CON H-, B is equal to aryl and X is -SO3- (-S-(CH2)7-CON H-C 6 H -S03-); A is equal to -(CH2)s- and X is a pyridinium group (-S-(CH2)s- CsH N + ). 

 The method for the detection of organic molecules in a sample comprising one or more organic compounds according to claim 2, wherein the gold core of the nanoparticles has a size between 1 and 5 nm. The method for the detection of organic molecules in a sample comprising one or more organic compounds according to claim 2, wherein the hydrodynamic radius is between 5 and 6 nm. The method for the detection of organic molecules in a sample comprising one or more organic compounds according to claim 1 , comprising the further steps of: optionally, characterizing the compound to be detected according to its diffusion coefficient, solubility in aqueous or organic solvent, and charge; selecting the functionalization of the nanoparticle suitable for interacting with the compound to be detected; dissolving the sample being examined in a deuterated aqueous or organic solvent or adding a deuterated aqueous or organic solvent to the sample being examined; optionally centrifuging the sample for removing insoluble species or macromolecules; adding the selected functionalized Au nanoparticles with residues of general formula (I) to the sample; and calculating the concentration of the organic molecule to be detected by integrating the signals related to the same detected by the magnetic resonance spectrum recorded and comparison with a calibration curve obtained with a series of samples of known titre of the same. The method for the detection of organic molecules in a sample comprising one or more organic compounds according to claim 7, wherein the functionalized Au nanoparticles are added at a concentration of between 5 and 150 Î¼Î.  The method for detecting organic molecules in a sample comprising one or more organic compounds according to one of claims 1 to 1 2, wherein the organic compounds are soluble in aqueous or organic solvents and have: solubility in the solvent of choice of at least 0.5 mM for the determination based on "NOE pumping" and 0.1 mM for the determination based on STD; diffusion coefficient higher than 1 x 1 0 "10 m 2 /sec (PM < 2000) ; presence of a net charge (positive or negative), or of a group able to accept/donate hydrogen bonds.
